summary: Detect the installed Hermes prompt interface before native execution
description: Fix the live WSL acceptance blocker by detecting whether the installed Hermes CLI accepts prompts through -z or legacy -p, using that exact interface for native build/critic/repair commands, and failing doctor/preflight before retry loops when neither contract is supported.
acceptance: R-1: Hermes prompt capability detects current -z and legacy -p interfaces from the real executable help output.
R-2: Native Hermes build critic and repair commands use the detected prompt flag and reject incompatible binaries before consuming loop retries.
R-3: Doctor and agent-check expose Hermes prompt compatibility with focused regressions and the full local Linux gate green.
verification: python3 -m pytest tests/test_smoke.py -q -k 'hermes and (native or doctor or agent_check)'
